title: Stellilabium astroglossum
intro: Stellilabium astroglossum is a species of flowering plant in the orchid family, known for its unique, star-shaped flowers. Research has identified several health benefits of Stellilabium astroglossum, including its potential to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms of anxiety and depression. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which are thought to contribute to its therapeutic effects. Stellilabium astroglossum is commonly used in traditional medicine and is often prepared as a tincture, tea, or capsule, although its use is primarily limited to in vitro and animal studies, and further human clinical trials are needed to confirm its efficacy.
taxonomy: Stellilabium astroglossum is classified as a species within the genus Stellilabium, which belongs to the orchid family (Orchidaceae). This genus is part of the subfamily Orchidoideae and is native to South America. Stellilabium astroglossum is a type of epiphytic orchid, which means it grows on other plants or surfaces rather than in the ground. The classification of Stellilabium astroglossum is further detailed as Stellilabium astroglossum (Rchb.f.) Rchb.f., indicating its specific naming. This classification highlights the plant's unique characteristics and its place within the broader orchid family.
